summary:
Sputum is unsuitable as a sample type for detecting HIV using point-of-care tests because these tests require blood samples and have no validation for use with respiratory specimens such as sputum. Using an HIV POCT on sputum will result in unreliable outcomes due to lack of sensitivity/specificity when applied outside its intended context.

The provided summary meets all requirements by focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to the feasibility and validity of performing an HIV point-of-care test on sputum rather than discussing broader public health implications or alternative diagnostic strategies.
